english: gentlemen! Stg.; fellow, grógs-kyeu (grogs khye'u) play-fellow, play-mate Dzl.; dpúṅ-grogs (dpung grogs) fellow-combatant, brother in arms; ˳dúg-grogs ('dug grogs), resp. bźúgs-grogs (bzhugs grogs) inmate, fellow-lodger Mil., *daṅ-rog* (*dang rog*) W., (v. braṅ-sa (brang sa)) id.; also neighbour W., C.; dgá-grogs (dga' grogs), gtáṅ-grogs (gtang grogs), grogs (grogs) companion in life, spouse, husband, wife, grogs (grogs) mi (mi) rnyed (rnyed) she cannot get a husband Mil.; tse (tshe) ˳dií ('di'i) grogs-skál (grogs skal) a man's destination as to marriage, the matrimonial lot assigned by fate Glr.; ˳dód-grogs ('dod grogs), mdzá-grogs (mdza' grogs), bzáṅ-grogs (bzang grogs) C. one beloved, lover, sweet-heart, mál-grogs (mal grogs) resp. gzím-grogs (gzim grogs) bed-fellow (not only 'concubine' Cs.); dmág-grogs (dmag grogs) ally, confederate (in war), hence also: -- 3. assistant, fellow-labourer, lás-grogs (las grogs) journeyman, under-workman; grogs (grogs) byéd-pa (byed pa) to help; rgán-mo (rgan mo) mčód-rten (mchod rten) skúd-pai (skud pa'i) grogs (grogs) byas (byas) they helped the old woman in anointing the pyramid Dzl.; rtsíg-grogs (rtsig grogs) byéd-pa (byed pa) to help in building a house; at present in C a word of courteousness in making requests: *teṅ rog naṅ* (*teng rog nang*) be so kind as to show me; *naṅ rog dze* (*nang rog mdzad*) would you kindly give me; *dha ṅa toṅ rog dzo* (*da nga stong rog mdzod*) now please let me go! cf. rogs (rogs).

an inhabited place, a human habitation, house, village, town, brgyá-groṅ (brgya grong), stoṅ-groṅ (stong grong) a place of a hundred, of a thousand houses or house-holds (mí-kyim (mi khyim)). -- Comp. groṅ-kyér (grong khyer) 1. a large town, city, B. and C., groṅ-kyer (grong khyer) (gyi (gyi)) mčóg (mchog) chief city, capital Tar. 2. fig. place, scene, sphere, (e.g. this world is a scene of illusions Mil.) -- groṅ-graṅs (grong grangs) the number of houses in a village or town. -- groṅ-mčóg (grong mchog) Mil., groṅ-mčóg (grong mchog) ˳drím-pa ('drim pa), ˳gró-ba ('gro ba), rgyúg-pa (rgyug pa) one that wanders about among the peasantry as a fortune-teller; clerical charlatan, hedge-priest. -- groṅ-gtám (grong gtam) prob. = groṅ-tsíg (grong tshig) -- groṅ-rdál (grong rdal) (Lex. जनपद 'an extension of houses') a large town, also a suburb. -- groṅ-pa (grong pa) 1. W. a villager, peasant, 2. C. = groṅ (grong). -- gróṅ-po (grong po) = groṅ (grong) Mil. -- gróṅ-dpon (grong dpon) village-chief, Sch. -- gróṅ-mí (grong mi) peasant. -- groṅ-tsíg (grong tshig) Lex. provincialism. -- gróṅ-tso (grong tsho) village, borough. -- groṅ-bźis (grong gzhis) farm Sch. -- groṅ-yul (grong yul) village Mil.
C. col. for graṅ-ba (grang ba) cold, in Glr. occasionally.
1. belly, gród-tsil (grod tshil) suet. -- 2. col. stomach; of ruminating animals the first stomach or paunch. -- 3. a dried paunch, or bullock's stomach, for keeping oil etc. Glr.
disadvantageous, injurious, gron-čé (gron che) very noxious, gron-méd (gron med) harmless, innoxious Lex.
pf. of ˳gról-ba ('grol ba), as sbst. = मुक्ति the having been delivered, deliverance (from the pain of existence).

1. advice, counsel, gros (gros) ˳débs-pa ('debs pa) B. to give advice; gros (gros) byéd-pa (byed pa) B., *dós gyáb-če* (*dos rgyab che*) W., to consider, to deliberate; to resolve, decide; gros (gros) ˳drí-ba ('dri ba) to ask (a person's) advice, to consult (with one); grós-˳dri-sa (gros 'dri sa) the place where advice may be asked, an oracle Glr.; grós-pa (gros pa) adviser, counsellor, senator; grós-mi (gros mi) id., head-man of a village; gros (gros) mtún-par (mthun par) by unanimous decree, unanimously Dzl. -- 2. speech, talk, = gtam (gtam) Mil. nt. -- 3. council (?). -- 4. Cs.: care, heed, caution, grós-ćan (gros can) careful, cautious, grós-med (gros med) careless, heedless.

pay, wages, fee, gla (gla) zá-ba (za ba) to live on wages, to work for daily wages Dzl.; gla-ltó (gla lto) food and wages; glá-pa (gla pa), glá-bo (gla bo) (col.), gla-mí (gla mi) a day-labourer, hired workman, glá-mo (gla mo) (Cs. glá-pa-mo (gla pa mo)) fem.

1. the musk-deer, Moschus moschiferus, glá-mo (gla mo) the female of it, gla-trúg (gla phrug) the young of it; glá-bai (gla ba'i) lté-ba (lte ba) musk-bag (lit. navel); glá-rtsi (gla rtsi) (W. *lar-si* (*lar si*)), Ssk. कस्तूरी musk, glá-rtsi-me-tog (gla rtsi me tog) Pedicularis megalantha, *gla-dá-ra* (*gla da ra*) W. Delphinium moschatum, two alpine plants smelling strongly of musk; gla-sgáṅ (gla sgang) n. of a medicinal root Cs.; gla-glád (gla glad) v. glaṅ-glad (glang glad). -- 2. n. of a pretty large tree, similar to, or the same as stár-bu (star bu) Glr.
eagle, vulture; glag (glag) krá-mo (khra mo) Sch., *lag-kyi* (*lag kyi*) W. (an eagle which is said to bark like <
